summary.intent: Fix direct-linking to older pipeline runs that were being filtered out when URL parameters opened in a new tab with default 24h time range instead of the needed 48h+ range.
summary.outcome: Backend fix was implemented and committed (f5c9df7d), build succeeded and deployed after manual rollout restart. Deployment configuration was updated to add OpenShift ImageStream trigger annotations for automatic redeployment on new image pushes.

repo: art-dashboard uses OpenShift BuildConfig to build images and apps/v1 Deployment (not DeploymentConfig) for deployment
Pipeline runs have time range filters that can hide older runs when URL parameters are used with default time ranges

code.finding: Added image.openshift.io/triggers annotation to enable automatic rollout when ImageStreamTag updates

workflow: OpenShift apps/v1 Deployments don't auto-rollout on new image pushes by default - imagePullPolicy: Always only pulls on pod creation
Manual 'kubectl rollout restart' needed after new image build completes unless ImageStream triggers are configured
Image digest verification can be done with 'kubectl get pod -o jsonpath' to check if deployment picked up new image

friction: Pre-commit hook required unsandboxed access and took 80+ seconds to complete
Deployment didn't auto-rollout after build completed, requiring manual intervention to restart deployment
New image was built but old pod continued running with stale image digest until manual restart
open_items: Verify ImageStream trigger annotation works correctly on next image build
Confirm ImageStream resource exists and is properly configured for art-dashboard
